sections.text: Teams & Key Users Region Key People Primary Focus Main Objects US / Global Aaron Sultan, Amanda Hyland, Greg Stuart, Shauna Peck, Erin Disney, Denise Champion, Alec Foster, and ~30 others Contacts, campaigns (events/webinars), lead routing, membership management Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Campaigns APAC Rohit Dadwal (MD), Nam Nguyen, Shanti Tolani,

sections.title: Ground Rules
sections.text: 🚫 Do not manually convert Leads. MMA runs a daily automated script that converts leads to contacts. If you manually convert a lead, you may create duplicates or break the automation. Coordinate with Aaron Sultan if you have questions about a specific lead. 🚫 Do not edit the UNKNWN account. This is a system placeholder account for unmatched leads. C

sections.title: What Is an Account?
sections.text: In Salesforce, an Account is a company — a brand, agency, tech platform, media company, or any organization that has a relationship with MMA. Every person (Contact) must belong to an Account. Every membership renewal (Opportunity) is tied to an Account. If you want to know whether a company is a member, you look at their Account record. MMA has tho

sections.title: Membership Status
sections.text: The most important field on an Account is Status__c . This is the primary membership filter used across all MMA reports, scripts, and data exports. 💡 The golden rule: To find all members, filter Status__c LIKE 'Active%' . Any Status__c value beginning with "Active" means the company is a current member.

sections.title: Status__c Values Explained
sections.text: Active-Parent The main (parent) membership. This company holds the primary membership record. Most global and national members fall here. Active-Subsidiary A subsidiary or child company covered under a parent company's membership. They get member benefits through the parent. Active-Subsidiary (irrelevant) Covered under a parent, but classified as "

sections.title: Supporting Membership Fields
sections.text: Field API Name Purpose Account Type Account_Type__c Broader category: Corporate Membership , Academic Membership , Local Membership , or Non-Member Membership Type Membership_Type__c Tier of membership: Global , Regional , Local Council , Strategic Partner , or Non-Member Next Renewal Date Next_Renewal_Date__c Date when this company's membership is

sections.title: Account Hierarchy: Parent & Child Accounts
sections.text: Large companies often have multiple Salesforce records — a parent company and one or more subsidiary accounts. This is called an Account Hierarchy. Example: A Large Holding Company Imagine a major holding company like a global advertising group. In Salesforce it might look like: Parent Account: "GlobalAdGroup Inc." — Status: Active-Parent — holds t

sections.title: Region
sections.text: Every Account has a Region__c field that determines which MMA regional team manages the relationship and which data exports/reports include the account. Region Value Covers Key MMA Team NA United States, Canada Aaron Sultan, Amanda Hyland, Greg Stuart Global Multi-region or globally scoped members Greg Stuart, US/Global team APAC Asia-Pacific (Sing

sections.title: Ecosystem (Industry Type)
sections.text: The Primary_Ecosystem__c field on Account classifies what type of company a member is within the marketing and media industry. It drives segmentation for outreach, reporting, and membership composition analysis. As of 2026, MMA has 1,016 active member accounts across all ecosystems. 🧭 The most important segmentation in Salesforce: Every active memb

sections.title: The Core Divide: Marketer vs. Solution Provider
sections.text: Single ecosystem value Marketer A company whose marketing team is an internal function — they spend money on advertising and media to promote their own brand, products, or services to consumers. They are the ones buying the marketing ecosystem's outputs. Ford, Coca-Cola, Bank of America, Marriott, Walmart, American Express, GEICO, Eli Lilly, L'Orea

sections.title: Ecosystem Values — Definitions & Examples
sections.text: Marketer Brands and advertisers. Their marketing team is internal — they buy media and run campaigns to sell their own products or services. Spans retail, CPG, financial services, healthcare, auto, hospitality, and more. The largest single ecosystem in MMA's membership. Agency Companies that manage marketing on behalf of brands. Includes media agen

sections.title: Edge Cases & Nuances
sections.text: The ecosystem field was assigned manually by people who understood each company's primary role. A few patterns worth knowing: Same company, different ecosystem depending on the entity Walmart (the retailer) = Marketer — they advertise to consumers to drive store traffic. Walmart Connect = Seller — they sell ad inventory to brands on the Walmart pla

Account Type Account_Type__c Corporate, Academic, Local, or Non-Member category Membership Type Membership_Type__c Global, Regional, Local Council, Strategic Partner, or Non-Member tier Primary Ecosystem Primary_Ecosystem__c Industry/company type (M

sections.title: Key Roles — Leadership Lookups
sections.text: MMA tracks named executives at each member account using dedicated Key Role fields on the Account object. Each field is a lookup that points directly to a Contact record — verified and maintained by Shauna. These fields are the authoritative way to identify CMOs, CEOs, and other C-suite contacts at member companies. 💡 Use Key Roles, not Title text,

sections.title: How to Pull CMOs for a Member Segment (Python — 2-step pattern)
sections.text: SOQL can't filter Contact mailable criteria through a Key Role relationship traversal in a single query. Use this two-step approach: # Step 1 — get active Marketer accounts with CMO populated accounts = sf.query(""" SELECT Id, Name, CMO__c, CMO__r.FirstName, CMO__r.LastName, CMO__r.Email FROM Account WHERE Status__c LIKE 'Active%' AND Region__c IN

sections.title: Special Accounts to Know
sections.text: 🚫 UNKNWN Account — This is a system placeholder. Leads and contacts that can't be matched to a real company are temporarily placed here. Do NOT manually move contacts to or from this account. The daily automation script manages this queue. Talk to Aaron Sultan if you see a contact here that should be matched to a real account. ⚠️ Admin Holding Acco

sections.title: How to Find Member Accounts
sections.text: When building a list view or report of member companies, always filter on Status__c : SOQL Filter for Active Members WHERE Status__c LIKE 'Active%' This catches all four Active status values: Active-Parent, Active-Subsidiary, Active-Subsidiary (irrelevant), and Active-Local Council. SOQL Filter for NA/Global Members Only WHERE Status__c LIKE 'Activ

sections.title: Dues Tier Structure
sections.text: Member dues are stored directly in Salesforce and are structured by ecosystem and revenue band. Each account is assigned a dues tier based on what kind of company they are and how large they are — the tier then determines their annual membership fee. Field API Name Notes 2026 Dues Charged X2026_Dues_Charged__c Actual dues billed for the current mem

sections.title: What Is a Contact?
sections.text: In Salesforce, a Contact is an individual person — an executive, marketer, account manager, or any employee at a member or non-member company. Every Contact must be linked to an Account (their company). Contacts without an Account cannot exist in the standard Salesforce data model. Contacts are the primary audience for MMA's email outreach, event i

sections.title: Key Contact Fields
sections.text: Field Label API Name Notes First Name FirstName Contact's first name Last Name LastName Contact's last name Email Email Primary email address — required for outreach Title Title Job title at their company Phone Phone Direct phone number Account Name Account.Name / AccountId The company this person belongs to — ALWAYS required Is Member Is_Member__c

sections.title: What Makes a Contact "Mailable"?
sections.text: When building lists for email campaigns or event invitations, a contact must meet all of the following criteria to be included. This is often called the "mailable" definition at MMA. Mailable Criteria Checklist ✅ Active Account: Their Account's Status__c must start with "Active" (Active-Parent, Active-Subsidiary, Active-Local Council, etc.) ✅ NA or

sections.title: Board Member Flags
sections.text: Board membership is tracked at the Contact level. MMA has several boards, and each has its own field. This matters for outreach — board members typically receive personal, individually crafted communications rather than mass emails. Is Board Member (catch-all) Is_Board_Member__c True if the contact is on ANY of the following boards: Global, NA, Med

sections.title: Website Login Tracking
sections.text: MMA tracks whether contacts have logged into MMAGlobal.com — the member portal. This helps identify engaged vs. inactive members. Field What It Means Has_website_login__c = 'Yes' This contact has an MMAGlobal.com account and has logged in at least once Total_website_logins__c How many times they've logged in total (lifetime). Higher = more engaged.

sections.title: How to Search for a Contact
sections.text: Use the global search bar at the top of Salesforce. Type the person's name or email address. Salesforce will show matches across all objects — click the result under "Contacts." If searching by email domain (e.g., all contacts at Nike), go to Contacts list views and filter by Email contains 'nike.com' . For large searches, use Reports — navigate to

sections.title: What to Do If a Contact Is on the Wrong Account
sections.text: Sometimes a contact ends up on the wrong Account — for example, they appear under "UNKNWN" or an old company they no longer work for. Here's how to handle it: Steps to Reassign a Contact Open the Contact record. Click Edit (or the edit pencil on the Account Name field). In the "Account Name" field, search for and select the correct company Account.

sections.title: Pardot / Account Engagement — Deliverability Fields
sections.text: Pardot (now called Account Engagement ) syncs bidirectionally with Salesforce. It writes bounce and opt-out signals back to Contact fields. Understanding which fields are authoritative — and which are Pardot-specific convenience fields — is important for accurate segmentation.

sections.title: Canonical Mailable Filters (use these)
sections.text: Field API Name Notes Email Opt Out HasOptedOutOfEmail Standard Salesforce field. Set when a contact unsubscribes via Pardot or is manually opted out. This is the field used in the mailable definition. Is Email Bounced IsEmailBounced Standard Salesforce field. Set to true when Pardot records a hard bounce. Use this — not the Pardot-specific hard bou

sections.title: Pardot-Specific Sync Fields (reference only)
sections.text: 💡 These fields are written by Pardot and are useful for understanding the Pardot state of a contact, but do not substitute them for the canonical SF fields above when building SOQL filters or Python scripts. Use IsEmailBounced and HasOptedOutOfEmail as your primary deliverability gates. Field API Name Notes Account Engagement Hard Bounced pi__pardo

sections.title: Granular Opt-Out Fields
sections.text: Beyond the global opt-out, MMA tracks category-specific opt-outs that let contacts unsubscribe from particular types of communications without opting out entirely. Field API Name Notes Promotions Opt-Out Promotions_opt_out__c Opted out of promotional/marketing emails specifically Surveys Opt-Out Surveys_opt_out__c Opted out of survey and research i

sections.title: DAMit Certification
sections.text: DAMit (Data-Driven Advertising & Marketing Intelligence Test) is MMA's annual marketing certification program. Contacts who pass earn a DAMit Certificate for that year. This is tracked in Salesforce with per-year boolean fields on the Contact object. Field API Name What It Means DAMit Certificate 2025 DAMit_Certificate_2025__c True = contact earned

sections.title: Newsletter Engagement — RASA & DATT
sections.text: MMA uses two newsletter/content delivery systems — RASA and DATT — that sync engagement data back to Salesforce. These fields track email opens, clicks, content preferences, and engagement scores at the contact level. They are supplemental engagement signals, not substitutes for the mailable definition. ⚠️ These fields are still being refined. RASA

sections.title: RASA — AI Newsletter Engagement
sections.text: RASA is an AI-powered newsletter personalization platform that delivers curated content to contacts. Engagement and preference data syncs back to Salesforce. Field API Name Notes Rasa Newsletter RasaNewsletter__c True = contact is subscribed to the main MMA RASA newsletter Rasa Newsletter DATT RasaNewsletterDATT__c True = contact is subscribed to t

sections.title: DATT — Data Analytics & Technology Newsletter
sections.text: DATT is MMA's Data Analytics & Technology newsletter. Engagement metrics from DATT also sync to Salesforce, in parallel with RASA. Field API Name Notes DATT Engagement Level DATT_Engagement_Level__c Engagement score for DATT newsletter activity DATT Engagement Update DATT_Engagement_Update__c When the DATT engagement score was last updated DATT Cli

sections.title: Engagement Tracking
sections.text: Several fields on Contact track when a contact last engaged with MMA events, webinars, or content. These are useful for filtering "recently active" contacts or surfacing who may need re-engagement outreach. Field API Name Notes Engagement Level Engagement_level__c Formula field. Values: Less than 6 mos (actively engaged), Over 12 mos (lapsing), Ina

sections.title: Event Outreach Tags
sections.text: These fields are used by the membership and events teams to flag contacts for targeted outreach — speaker nominations, CMO-tier invitations, and specific list segments. Field API Name Notes Seed List Tag Seed_List_Tag__c Picklist that tags a contact for a specific outreach segment. Common values: 2026 Tier 1 CMOs , 2026 Tier 2 CMOs , Member CMOs no

sections.title: Creating a New Contact
sections.text: If you need to add someone to Salesforce who doesn't exist yet: First search for them — they may already exist as a Lead or under a different account. Navigate to the Contacts tab and click New . Fill in: First Name, Last Name, Email, Title, and — critically — the Account Name (their company). If their company doesn't exist as an Account yet, you'l

sections.title: Contact Type
sections.text: The Contact Type field ( Contact_Type_1__c ) marks contacts who hold a named formal role at their member account — the billing rep, the main membership contact, a board director, a council member. These are the people Shauna and the membership team specifically place and maintain. Never bulk-move, merge, or auto-process contacts where this field is

sections.title: Account Rep Values
sections.text: Contact Type Value What It Means Account-Level Mirror Main Rep Primary MMA membership contact at the account Main_Rep__c on Account Secondary Rep Secondary membership contact Secondary_Rep__c on Account Billing Contact Finance/AP contact for dues billing — receives invoices — LATAM Rep Regional rep for the Latin America chapter — EMEA Rep Regional

sections.title: Board & Council Values
sections.text: All BOD (Board of Directors) values track the contact's role across MMA's global and regional boards. Council values track community think tank membership. BOD Values BOD Global · BOD NA · BOD Media · BOD CDO · BOD APAC · BOD EMEA · BOD LATAM · BOD MENA BOD Argentina · BOD Brazil · BOD China · BOD Germany · BOD India · BOD Indonesia BOD Mexico · BO

sections.title: Contact Type vs. Is_Board_Member__c
sections.text: ⚠️ These two fields are not the same and don't fully overlap. Is_Board_Member__c catches most board roles but does NOT capture Billing Contact, Main Rep, Secondary Rep, or country-level BODs. Contact_Type_1__c captures all of those, but you still need Is_Board_Member__c for the catch-all. For any exclusion in automation, always filter on BOTH. Cont

sections.title: What Is a Lead?
sections.text: In Salesforce, a Lead is an unconfirmed person — someone who has shown interest in MMA but hasn't been verified and matched to a company Account yet. Leads are a temporary state. The end goal is always to convert a lead into a Contact linked to the correct Account. Think of leads as the "waiting room" of Salesforce. When someone fills out a form on

sections.title: Where Do Leads Come From?
sections.text: Leads arrive in Salesforce through several channels, tracked in the LeadSource field: Lead Source How It Gets Into SF Common Examples Web Forms Salesforce Web-to-Lead forms on MMAGlobal.com push directly to SF POSSIBLE conference registrations, "Contact Us" submissions, research download forms Webinar Registrations Webinar platform (Zoom, GoTo) reg

sections.title: Custom Lead Fields From Forms
sections.text: Field API Name Purpose Web Form Name Web_To_Lead_Forms__c Which form on the website the person submitted Primary Ecosystem Primary_Ecosystem__c What type of company they work for (pre-filled from form dropdowns) Global Market Global_Market__c Their geographic market/region Comments / Questions Comments_or_Questions__c Free-text field from the form

sections.title: Lead Status Values
sections.text: Every Lead has a Status field that tracks where it is in the review process. Status Meaning What's Next New Just arrived in Salesforce — not yet reviewed by anyone Daily script will attempt auto-conversion. If no match, stays as New. Working Someone is actively reviewing or attempting to identify/contact this lead Update notes, try to identify comp

sections.title: The Daily Auto-Conversion Process
sections.text: MMA runs a Python script called lead_convert_prep.py every morning. This script is the right way to handle lead conversion at scale. Here's how it works: 1 Script runs each morning The automation queries all leads with Status = New or Working and reviews them for conversion eligibility. 2 Company matching The script compares the lead's Company Name

sections.title: What You Can Do With Leads
sections.text: Even though you shouldn't convert leads manually, there are things you CAN do: View leads — Open the Leads tab to see incoming inquiries. You can read their details, form responses, and UTM data. Update the Status — If you've reviewed a lead and confirmed it's spam or unqualified, you can set Status to Unqualified . Add notes — Use the Notes & Atta

sections.title: Leads vs. Contacts: Key Differences
sections.text: Dimension Lead Contact Account relationship None — not linked to any account Always linked to an Account Membership status Unknown — leads have no membership Inherits membership from Account Can receive Pardot emails? Limited — leads can be in campaigns but Pardot email is Contact-based Yes, fully Campaign membership Can be campaign members as Lead

sections.title: What Is a Campaign?
sections.text: In Salesforce, a Campaign is a record that represents any organized MMA activity — an event, a webinar, an email send, a survey, or a content download. Campaigns are how MMA tracks engagement: who registered, who attended, who downloaded something, who was just invited. Every time someone interacts with a Campaign (registers for a webinar, attends

sections.title: Campaign Naming Convention
sections.text: All campaigns follow this format: REGION _ TYPECODE _ YYYYMMDD _ EventName

sections.title: Region Codes
sections.text: Code Meaning ALL Global / all regions NAM North America APAC Asia-Pacific EMEA Europe, Middle East & Africa LATAM Latin America

sections.title: Type Codes
sections.text: Code Event Type Wbnr Webinar (virtual) Wbs Workshop or Session (in-person or hybrid) Eml Email campaign / outreach Tsh Thought leadership or special content

sections.title: Real Examples
sections.text: ALL_Wbnr_20260408_TheStateOfMarketingAnalytics APAC_Wbs_20260408_MMAIndonesiaDataInsights NAM_Eml_20260408_SMARTIES2026 ALL_Wbs_20260429_POSSIBLE2026_MFCandB2BCouncilLunch Note: The event name portion uses CamelCase (no spaces). For multi-word events with sub-events (like POSSIBLE sessions), the parent event name comes first.

sections.title: Campaign Hierarchy (Parent & Child Campaigns)
sections.text: Large events like POSSIBLE often have a parent campaign record and many child campaign records for individual sessions. This hierarchy allows MMA to track both overall event attendance and session-level engagement. Example: POSSIBLE 2026 Parent Campaign: POSSIBLE2026_registration — tracks all registrants Child Campaign 1: ALL_Wbs_20260429_POSSIBLE2

sections.title: Campaign Member Statuses
sections.text: When a Contact or Lead is added to a Campaign, they become a Campaign Member with a Status. The status tells you what their relationship to the campaign is — did they attend? Just register? Were they invited but didn't show? Confirmed Engagement (attended / watched / completed) Attended Checked In Paid-Attended Attended - MMA Staff On-Demand Watche

sections.title: How to Add Someone to a Campaign
sections.text: 1 Open the Campaign record Navigate to the Campaigns tab and find the event you want to add someone to. You can search by the campaign name. 2 Scroll to the Campaign Members related list On the campaign record page, scroll down to find the "Campaign Members" related list. You may need to expand it. 3 Click "Add Members" Click the "Add Members" butt

sections.title: Campaign Status Lifecycle
sections.text: Status When to Use Planning Campaign has been created but the event isn't promoted yet. Still being set up. Active Event is live — invitations are sent, registrations are open. Members should be added now. Completed Event is over. Final attendance has been logged. No new members should be added. Aborted Event was cancelled or didn't happen. No furt

sections.title: What Is an Opportunity?
sections.text: An Opportunity in Salesforce represents a potential or confirmed deal between MMA and a company. At MMA, the three main types of deals tracked as Opportunities are: Membership Renewal When an existing member renews their annual membership. This is the most common type — every member account generates a renewal Opportunity each year. Sponsorship A c

sections.title: The Opportunity Pipeline (Stages)
sections.text: Every Opportunity moves through a series of stages from initial interest to payment received. This is the pipeline that sales and membership teams use to track deal progress. Prospecting → Qualified → Negotiation / Review → Commitments: Verbal / Written → Term Sheet Sent → Contract Sent → Invoice Sent → Closed Won (Payment Received) 💡 There is also

sections.title: Stage Descriptions
sections.text: Stage Meaning Who Acts Next Prospecting Initial identification — we think this company might renew or be a sponsor, but no conversation has happened yet MMA rep reaches out Qualified Confirmed that this deal is real and worth pursuing — the company has expressed interest MMA rep qualifies the deal details Negotiation/Review Both sides are discussin

sections.title: Key Opportunity Fields
sections.text: Field API Name Notes Opportunity Name Name Use format: "2026 APAC Local Council Renewal_CompanyName" or "2026 DMEXCO Sponsorship_CompanyName" Account AccountId The company this deal is with — required Stage StageName Current position in the pipeline — update as deal progresses Close Date CloseDate Expected date the deal will close (payment received

sections.title: Naming Conventions for Opportunities
sections.text: Good naming makes it easy to find opportunities in reports and list views. Use this pattern: Format {Year} {Region} {Type}_{CompanyName} Examples 2026 APAC Local Council Renewal_PT Telkom Indonesia 2026 EMEA Sponsorship_DMEXCO_CompanyName 2026 NA Membership Renewal_CompanyName 2026 LATAM Sponsorship_MercadoLibre

sections.title: Who Uses Opportunities by Region
sections.text: APAC — Rohit Dadwal & Team (Singapore, Indonesia, India, Vietnam, Sri Lanka) APAC uses Opportunities very heavily — it's the primary tool for managing the Local Council membership renewals and event sponsorships across the region. Typical APAC workflow: Create Opportunity at Prospecting stage when renewal season begins Advance through stages as con

sections.title: Opportunity Naming for Renewals: Year Matters
sections.text: Each year's renewal should be a new Opportunity record. Don't edit the prior year's closed Opportunity. Create a new one for the current year. This preserves the deal history and makes year-over-year reporting accurate. ⚠️ Tip: When a prior year's Opportunity is Closed Won, clone it (use the Clone button in Salesforce) to create the new year's rene

sections.title: Data Ownership Model
sections.text: MMA's Salesforce instance is a centrally managed, globally shared CRM . The US/Global Membership team is the primary steward of the system — they own the core data model, integration scripts, and data quality standards. Regional teams (APAC, EMEA, LATAM, MEA) own the contact and opportunity records for their geographic markets. Think of it this way

sections.title: How Contacts Enter Salesforce
sections.text: Contacts don't just appear — they come in through several defined pathways. Understanding these pathways helps you know whether to look for someone as a Contact, a Lead, or somewhere in between.

sections.title: The Five Entry Points
sections.text: 1. Web Forms → Leads → Auto-Conversion Every registration form, download form, and sign-up page on MMAGlobal.com is connected to Salesforce as a Web-to-Lead form . When someone submits a form, a Lead record is automatically created. A nightly automation script then runs and: Looks up the email against existing Contacts If a match is found, updates

sections.title: End-to-End Contact Flow (Web Form Path)
sections.text: 🌐 Web Form Person submits form on MMAGlobal.com → 📋 Lead Created Salesforce Web-to-Lead fires automatically → ⚙️ Nightly Script Auto-conversion runs overnight → 👤 Contact Linked to Account, added to Campaign → 📧 Pardot Sync Becomes mailable in Account Engagement

sections.title: RACI — Global Membership Team
sections.text: The following RACI defines responsibilities for the core data tasks that US/Global Membership team handles in Salesforce. R = Responsible , A = Accountable , C = Consulted , I = Informed . This RACI covers US/Global Membership only. Regional teams (APAC, EMEA, LATAM) have their own internal processes for regional tasks. Task Aaron Sultan Sales & Me

sections.title: Web Forms & Campaign Connections
sections.text: Every public-facing form at MMA — registration pages, download gates, survey links, event sign-ups — is built on Salesforce's Web-to-Lead framework and linked to a Campaign record. This is how MMA tracks who engaged with what, when. How It Works A new form is created in Salesforce as a Web-to-Lead form. The form is linked to a specific Campaign rec

sections.title: Data Quality Standards
sections.text: Salesforce is only as useful as the quality of the data inside it. These are MMA's current data quality rules — all staff are expected to follow them when creating or editing records.

sections.title: Contact Quality Rules
sections.text: Rule Why It Matters Every contact must be linked to an Account Orphaned contacts can't inherit membership status and won't appear in member mailing lists Email address is required for any new contact Without an email, the contact can never be reached or tracked in Pardot No duplicate contacts Duplicates inflate mailing lists, skew engagement report

sections.title: Helpful Data Quality Fields
sections.text: Field What It Flags Action Need_email_researched__c Contact is missing or has a bad email address Research correct email via ZoomInfo, LinkedIn, or Apollo Need_company_researched__c Contact name or company is unknown/placeholder Identify the person's real name and company, update the record Data_Quality_Score__c Composite score reflecting overall r

sections.title: Requesting Salesforce Access
sections.text: New MMA employees are set up with Salesforce access as part of IT onboarding. If you need access or need your permissions adjusted: New user setup — Contact IT/your manager. IT creates the Salesforce user account and assigns the appropriate profile. Read-only access — Standard for most non-membership staff. Can view all records but cannot edit. Edi

sections.title: Creating a Contact — Step by Step
sections.text: Follow these steps any time you need to manually add a new person to Salesforce. Most contacts arrive automatically via web forms and lead conversion — manual creation should be the exception, not the rule. 1 Search first Use the global search bar at the top of Salesforce. Search by full name AND by email address. Also check the Leads tab — the per

sections.title: Admin Training Requirements
sections.text: All Salesforce admins must complete ongoing training to maintain elevated access. These requirements ensure everyone with admin permissions is current on best practices, security, and platform capabilities. Admin Intermediate Trail One-Time ~11h 45m. Required once upon gaining admin access. Covers: Formula fields and validation rules Security model

sections.title: Requesting Salesforce Work
sections.text: All Salesforce work requests — from minor field updates to major process redesigns — must go through the proper channel. This keeps requests tracked, prioritized, and delivered with the appropriate level of quality control. 📬 Submit all requests by email: Send to aaron@mmaglobal.com and CC membership@mmaglobal.com . Once received, Global Support re

sections.title: Work Tiers & SLAs
sections.text: Every request is classified into one of four tiers based on complexity and impact. Tiers determine the SLA and the level of review required before changes go live. Tier Type What's Covered SLA Governance Tier 1 User Management Access, licenses, MFA, permission sets 1 business day (licenses up to 1 week) Regional admins manage access; Global Support

sections.title: Regional Board of Directors Fields
sections.text: In addition to the global board flags covered on the Contacts page , Salesforce tracks country-level board membership for each region. These fields are maintained by the respective regional CRM leads and are important for EMEA (Bülent Doruker) and APAC (Rohit Dadwal) communications. Region Contact Field Who Maintains EMEA — Turkey Current_BOD_Turke

sections.title: Regional Preference Center Fields
sections.text: Each region has its own Preference Center where contacts can manage what types of MMA communications they receive. These preferences sync from Pardot back to Salesforce. There are three preference categories per region. Region Programs & Events Research & Insights Smarties Awards NA / Global Preference_Ctr_Programs_NA__c Preference_Ctr_Research_NA_

sections.title: Cross-Region Collaboration
sections.text: 💡 Global campaigns: For global events like POSSIBLE that span regions, the campaign's Region__c is set to Global and the name prefix is ALL . Each region may also create their own sub-campaigns if needed for regional tracking. ⚠️ Don't filter out regional data unintentionally. If you're working on a global analysis and filtering on Region__c, make

sections.title: Who to Contact for SF Help

sections.title: Integration Ecosystem Overview
sections.text: MMA's Salesforce instance is the hub for a network of connected platforms. These integrations show up in Salesforce as custom field namespaces (e.g., pi__ for Pardot, LID__ for LinkedIn) or as fields explicitly created to track data flowing in from external tools. 📧 Pardot / Account Engagement Email Marketing Platform Active — Core 📤 Outreach Sales

sections.title: Integrations — Detailed Reference
sections.text: 📧 Pardot / Account Engagement Salesforce's marketing automation & email platform — MMA's primary outreach tool Active — Core Bidirectional Sync Native SF Integration Pardot (officially "Marketing Cloud Account Engagement") is MMA's email marketing platform. It is deeply integrated with Salesforce — every mailable Contact in SF has a corresponding P

sections.title: Automations & Custom Scripts
sections.text: Beyond the third-party integrations above, MMA runs a set of Python-based automation scripts that handle recurring data management tasks. These are maintained by Aaron Sultan (US/Global) and Bülent Doruker (EMEA automations). Script / Automation What It Does Frequency Owner Lead Auto-Conversion sf_lead_converter.py Pulls all open Leads, matches to

sections.title: Engagement Tracking Fields
sections.text: Multiple integrations contribute to a set of Contact-level engagement tracking fields that roll up a person's activity across all platforms. These are useful for segmentation and identifying highly engaged vs. disengaged contacts. Field What It Captures Source Last_event_participation__c Date of the most recent in-person event or webinar attendance

sections.title: Regional Preference Center Fields
sections.text: MMA gives contacts the ability to manage their communication preferences through region-specific Preference Centers. These preferences are captured in Pardot and synced back to Salesforce. Each region has its own set of fields. Region Programs Field Research & Insights Field Smarties Field NA / Global Preference_Ctr_Programs_NA__c Preference_Ctr_Re

sections.title: What Is Pardot?
sections.text: Pardot — officially rebranded as Salesforce Marketing Cloud Account Engagement — is MMA's primary email marketing platform. Every mass email sent to members (event invitations, newsletters, webinar promotions, research alerts) goes through Pardot. Pardot works with Salesforce contacts through a concept called Prospects . Every mailable contact in S

sections.title: Salesforce ↔ Pardot Sync
sections.text: Understanding the sync is critical. Changes in one system propagate to the other — but there are rules about which system "wins" when there's a conflict. How the Sync Works Every Salesforce Contact with an email address can sync to a Pardot Prospect. Contacts missing an email address are not synced. The sync runs continuously (near real-time). New

sections.title: Business Units — MMA's Pardot Setup
sections.text: MMA uses multiple Pardot Business Units (BUs) within a single Salesforce org. Each BU is a separate Pardot environment with its own lists, emails, and templates. Business Unit Used By Notes MMA NA (Primary) Jason, Dan — all North America and Global sends The main BU. Where most MMA email builds and sends happen. EMEA Bülent Doruker Separate BU for

sections.title: Lists
sections.text: Lists are the foundation of Pardot sending. Every email goes to one or more lists. Understanding the different list types prevents you from accidentally emailing the wrong people.

sections.title: List Types
sections.text: Type How It Works When to Use Static List A fixed set of prospects you manually add or import. Doesn't change automatically. One-off sends, event invitation lists, imports from CSV or Salesforce reports Dynamic List Automatically updates based on criteria you define (e.g., "all prospects where Account Status = Active-Parent and Region = NA"). Membe

sections.title: Creating a Static List
sections.text: 1 Navigate to Prospects > Lists In Pardot, go to the Lists menu under the Prospects tab. 2 Click "+ Add List" Give the list a clear, descriptive name. Follow the MMA naming convention: include the event/program name and date (e.g., CCS 2026 — Round 1 Invites ). 3 Set the list type Leave "Is Public" unchecked unless this list should appear in the Em

sections.title: Creating a Dynamic List
sections.text: 1 Go to Prospects > Lists > "+ Add List" Check the "Dynamic List" checkbox. This reveals the criteria builder. 2 Set criteria Add rules based on Prospect fields, CRM fields (Salesforce fields), or activity. Example: Prospect CRM field "Account Status" contains "Active" AND Prospect CRM field "Region__c" equals "NA" . 3 Save and verify count Pardot

sections.title: Email Templates
sections.text: Templates are reusable email layouts. Jason and Dan maintain MMA's template library. A template defines the visual structure — header, body regions, footer, unsubscribe link — and uses merge fields to personalize content at send time.

sections.title: Creating or Editing a Template
sections.text: 1 Go to Content > Email Templates Browse existing templates. MMA has templates for event invites, newsletters, webinar promos, and member communications. 2 Clone before editing Never edit an active template directly. Clone it first (Actions > Copy), then edit the copy. This preserves the original for historical sends. 3 Use the drag-and-drop editor

sections.title: Key Merge Fields
sections.text: What You Want Pardot Merge Tag Recipient's first name {{Recipient.FirstName}} Recipient's last name {{Recipient.LastName}} Recipient's full name {{Recipient.Name}} Recipient's company {{Recipient.Company}} Recipient's email address {{Recipient.Email}} Unsubscribe link (required by law) %%unsubscribe%% View-in-browser link %%view_online%% MMA sender

sections.title: Sending a List Email
sections.text: A List Email is a one-time send to one or more lists. This is the most common send type at MMA — event invitations, webinar promotions, member announcements, etc. Step-by-Step: Send a List Email 1 Go to Emails > List Emails > "+ Add List Email" This opens the email creation wizard. 2 Name the email Use a clear internal name that includes the event/

sections.title: Connecting Pardot Sends to Salesforce Campaigns
sections.text: Every Pardot email send can — and should — be connected to a Salesforce Campaign. This is how email engagement (opens, clicks) flows into Salesforce as CampaignMember records, enabling reporting and engagement tracking. How to Link a List Email to a Salesforce Campaign When creating the List Email, scroll to the Salesforce Campaign field in the ema

sections.title: Form Handlers
sections.text: A Form Handler connects an external web form (on MMAGlobal.com or any landing page) to Pardot. When someone submits the form, Pardot captures the submission, creates or updates the Prospect record, and can trigger automated actions. Form Handlers are different from Pardot Forms — a Form is hosted and served by Pardot, while a Form Handler captures

sections.title: Creating a Form Handler
sections.text: 1 Go to Content > Form Handlers > "+ Add Form Handler" Give it a descriptive name that matches the form it's connected to. 2 Configure field mappings Map the form fields to Pardot Prospect fields. For example: form field "email" → Prospect field "Email" , "first_name" → "First Name" , "company" → "Company" . The form field names must match exactly

sections.title: Completion Actions
sections.text: Completion Actions fire immediately when a form is submitted. They are the most direct way to trigger Pardot and Salesforce updates from a form fill. Completion Action What It Does Add to List Adds the submitting prospect to a specified Pardot list Remove from List Removes from a list (useful for unsubscribe forms) Add to Salesforce Campaign Create

sections.title: Automation Rules
sections.text: Automation Rules run on a schedule and apply bulk actions to prospects that match defined criteria. Unlike Completion Actions (which fire once on a specific event), Automation Rules continuously check your prospect database and apply actions to any matching records. Setting Options & Notes Run type Repeat — runs continuously, re-evaluating prospect

sections.title: Engagement Studio
sections.text: Engagement Studio is Pardot's visual drip-sequence builder. Use it when you want to send a series of emails over time with branching logic — for example, send a webinar reminder, wait 3 days, check if they opened it, then send a different follow-up based on whether they did or didn't.

sections.title: When to Use Engagement Studio vs. List Emails
sections.text: Use List Email when... Use Engagement Studio when... Sending a single one-time email to a list Sending a sequence of 2+ emails over days or weeks A simple blast with no follow-up logic You need "if they opened → do X, if they didn't → do Y" branching Speed matters — setting up is faster Nurture sequences, onboarding flows, multi-touch event promoti

sections.title: Building an Engagement Studio Program
sections.text: 1 Go to Automation > Engagement Studio > "+ Add Program" Name the program clearly. Set the recipient list(s) and any suppression lists upfront. 2 Build the flow Drag-and-drop elements: Send Email (pick a template), Wait (pause X days), Trigger (did they open? click? visit a page?), Action (add to list, update a field, notify a user). 3 Test the pro

sections.title: Reporting & Tracking
sections.text: After sending, Pardot tracks opens, clicks, unsubscribes, and bounces. Here's where to find what you need. What You Want to See Where to Find It in Pardot Open rate, click rate, bounce rate for a specific send Emails > List Emails → click the email name → Reports tab Who clicked a specific link Email report > Clicks section > expand the link Who un

sections.title: MMA-Specific Conventions
sections.text: A few naming and process conventions the team follows to keep Pardot organized. Naming Convention — Lists Format: [Program/Event] — [Purpose] — [YYYY-MM-DD if dated] CCS 2026 — Round 1 Invites CCS 2026 — Already Registered (Suppress) POSSIBLE 2026 — All Attendees Webinar — Mailable Marketer Members — Dynamic Naming Convention — List Emails Format:

sections.title: List Scoring, Rule Groups & Segmentation Rules
sections.text: Beyond basic list types, Pardot has a built-in scoring system and advanced criteria-building tools. Understanding these makes you a much more precise sender.

sections.title: Prospect Scoring
sections.text: Every prospect in Pardot has a score that increases as they engage with MMA's content. Higher score = more engaged contact. Action Points Notes Email click +3 pts Any link click in a Pardot-sent email File / content access +3 pts Accessing a Pardot-tracked file download or landing page Form submission +10 pts Submitting a Pardot form or form handle

sections.title: Rule Groups in Dynamic Lists
sections.text: When building dynamic list criteria, Pardot uses AND logic within a rule group and OR logic between groups . This lets you build complex "this AND that, OR this other combination" audiences without splitting into multiple lists. Example: Multi-Condition Dynamic List Goal: All mailable NA Marketer members, OR any board member who is also a Marketer

sections.title: Segmentation Rules
sections.text: Segmentation Rules are similar to Automation Rules, but they run only once . Use them when you want to perform a one-time bulk action on a defined segment — for example, adding all contacts who clicked a specific link in a past email to a new list. Once run, they don't re-evaluate new prospects. Segmentation Rules are useful for historical cleanup

sections.title: Disengaged Contacts & Deliverability
sections.text: One of the most important operational concepts in Pardot: disengaged contacts — prospects who haven't interacted with an email in 6 or more months — require careful handling to protect MMA's sender reputation and overall email deliverability. Prospect Status Definition Risk if Ignored Engaged Has opened, clicked, or otherwise interacted with a Pard

sections.title: Email Best Practices
sections.text: Apply these best practices to every MMA email campaign, regardless of audience size.

sections.title: Subject Lines
sections.text: 50–65 characters is the sweet spot — displays fully in most desktop and mobile clients without truncation Front-load the key message — the most important word should come first, not last, since mobile clients truncate early Personalize when possible — adding the recipient's first name or company can lift open rates Avoid spam-trigger words — FREE,

sections.title: Pre-Header Text
sections.text: The pre-header is the preview snippet that appears after the subject line in most inboxes. It should complement the subject — not repeat it. Keep it under 100 characters. If you leave it blank, email clients pull the first visible line of your email body (which is often the unsubscribe link or an image alt tag).

sections.title: Content Length
sections.text: Email Type Target Word Count Why Event invite / cold outreach 100–150 words Get to the point. Busy executives won't scroll through a wall of text. Newsletter / member update 250–300 words Subscribers expect more content — still keep it scannable with headers and bullets. Reminder / follow-up 50–100 words Shorter is better for reminders — just the C

sections.title: Responsive Design (Mobile-First)
sections.text: Single-column layout — multi-column side-by-side designs break on mobile and should be avoided Minimum 14px font size for body text — anything smaller is hard to read without zooming on a phone CTA buttons at least 44px tall — needs to be thumb-friendly on touchscreens Images under 200kb — large images load slowly on mobile data; always include des

sections.title: Calls to Action (CTAs)
sections.text: One primary CTA above the fold — visible without scrolling; this is the action you most want them to take One secondary CTA in the body — reinforces the action for readers who scroll down Use styled button elements , not plain text links, for the primary CTA — they're more visible, more clickable, and easier to track Action-oriented CTA text — "Reg

sections.title: Segmentation & Personalization
sections.text: Segment before you send — a Marketer member and a Solution Provider should receive tailored messaging, not the same generic blast; the same event appeals differently to each ecosystem Use variable tags for personalization: {{Recipient.FirstName}} , {{Recipient.Company}} , etc. Always test merge fields — send a test to an account with known field va

sections.title: Completion Actions
sections.text: Completion Actions fire automatically when a prospect takes a specific action (clicks a link, opens an email, submits a form). They're one of the most useful Pardot tools for post-send automation. Trigger Common Completion Actions Email click Add to list, add to Salesforce Campaign (with status "Responded"), change a field value, notify a user Emai

sections.title: Send Timing
sections.text: Best days: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday Best time: 9–11am Eastern — catches East Coast at the start of the workday while still being early enough on the West Coast Avoid: Mondays (post-weekend inbox overload), Fridays (people checking out early), major US holidays and holiday windows Global sends: NA BU uses Eastern as the baseline; EMEA and APAC s

sections.title: Compliance
sections.text: Every email MMA sends must comply with applicable email marketing laws. Three regulations govern MMA's audience: CAN-SPAM (US), GDPR (EU/UK), and CASL (Canada). All three require the same core behaviors — an easy opt-out, accurate sender identity, physical address, and prompt honoring of unsubscribes. 🇺🇸 CAN-SPAM (US) Requires a clear unsubscribe m

sections.title: Reports vs. Dashboards — What's the Difference
sections.text: Salesforce has two distinct tools for viewing data. They're related but serve different purposes: 📊 Dashboards Visual summaries — charts, graphs, and metric tiles built on top of reports. Great for a quick at-a-glance read on how something is trending. Dashboards refresh on demand but don't show individual records. You go to a dashboard to understa

sections.title: Key Dashboards
sections.text: These are the dashboards the US team uses actively. All links go directly to Salesforce — you must be logged in. Event dashboards 2026 Events The main events tracking dashboard for the current year. Covers all 2026 events — CCS, CATS, roundtables, POSSIBLE, and others. Shows registration counts, attendance rates, status breakdowns (Attended, Regist

sections.title: Key Report Folders
sections.text: Reports are organized into folders in Salesforce. These are the ones most relevant for day-to-day US team work: Folder Owner / Purpose Link 2026 Key Audience Lists Angela Gray's folder — the official 2026 mailing audiences. Contains CMO lists, main rep lists, prospect tiers, and Key Role pulls by ecosystem. Start here when you need an approved outr

All are actively maintained. 📌 Current boards — Jade Public Reports The primary board member roster for the US team. Covers all active board members across Global Board, NA Board, Media Board, and CDO Board. Named after Jade

These live in Angela's 2026 Key Audience Lists folder. 📣 Member CMO [Marketers NON Board] 2026 Key Audience Lists CMO Key Role contacts at Marketer member accounts, excluding board members. The standard CCS/events invite list for senior Marketer out

This is the report that powers the mailable count snapshot tracking. Use it when you need a historical view of how the database has grown or shrunk over time. Open report 🏢 All NAGL Member Companies Public Reports A cl

The most common ones: 📋 Main 2026 Events AS Events Dashboard The master events report for all 2026 CCS, CATS, roundtable, and POSSIBLE sessions combined. All statuses, all attendees, all campaign members in one place. Open report ✅ Status-

sections.title: Webinar Reports
sections.text: The Webinar Reports folder (122 reports) covers all MMA webinar programs. The most recently active ones as of April 2026: 🤖 Decoding AI series reports Webinar Reports Multiple reports covering the Decoding AI for Marketers training series across all years (2023–2026). Sliced by: registrants, ecosystem, member status, company, title, UTM source/medi

sections.title: Shauna's Reports — Membership Baselines
sections.text: Shauna Peck maintains a small, clean folder called Membership Misc (Shauna) with 6 reliable baseline reports — one per ecosystem. These are simple, well-structured reports useful when you need a clean member list to hand off or reference: Report Name What It Contains Active Parent Members — ALL ECOSYSTEMS All active-parent NA/Global member accounts

sections.title: Tips for Working with Reports
sections.text: 🔍 Finding a report: Go to the Reports tab in Salesforce → search by name. If you're not sure of the exact name, filter by folder. Most key reports live in Public Reports, AS Events Dashboard, or 2026 Key Audience Lists. 📥 Exporting: From any report, click the dropdown arrow next to "Edit" → "Export" → choose Excel format. Always check the row count

sections.title: How Do I...
sections.text: 🔍 Find if a company is a member 1 Search for the company name in the Salesforce global search bar 2 Open the Account record 3 Look at the Status__c field — any value starting with "Active" = member 4 Check Next_Renewal_Date__c to see when they renew 👤 Find all contacts at a company 1 Search for the company name in global search 2 Open the Account r

sections.title: Status Values at a Glance
sections.text: Account Status (Status__c) Active-Parent Main membership holder Active-Subsidiary Covered under parent Active-Subsidiary (irrelevant) Covered, low engagement Active-Local Council APAC local member Pending Deal in progress Inactive/Cancelled Former member Non-Member No membership Campaign Status Planning Being set up — not yet live Active Open for r

sections.text: Format REGION _ TYPECODE _ YYYYMMDD _ EventName Token Options REGION ALL (global) | NAM (North America) | APAC | EMEA | LATAM TYPECODE Wbnr (Webinar) | Wbs (Workshop/Session) | Eml (Email) | Tsh (Thought Leadership) YYYYMMDD Date of the event. Example: 20260429 = April 29, 2026 EventName CamelCase, no spaces. Keep concise. For sub-events, put paren

sections.title: Key Fields Quick Reference

Account Next_Renewal_Date__c Date field What type of company is this? Account Primary_Ecosystem__c Marketer, Agency, Enabler, etc. Which region is this account in? Account Region__c NA,

sections.title: Dos and Don'ts
